questions.question: What is the modulus q used in both FN-DSA-512 and FN-DSA-1024?
questions.explanation: Both FN-DSA parameter sets use q = 12289. This prime is chosen because q ≡ 1 (mod 2n) for both n=512 and n=1024, enabling efficient NTT/FFT operations.

questions.question: What is the average signature size for FN-DSA-512?
questions.explanation: FN-DSA-512 produces signatures averaging 666 bytes. This is the smallest among NIST's PQC signature standards, about 3.6× smaller than ML-DSA-44's 2,420 bytes.

questions.question: Which NIST security category does FN-DSA-512 target?
questions.explanation: FN-DSA-512 targets NIST Category 1, providing security equivalent to AES-128. FN-DSA-1024 targets Category 5 (AES-256 equivalent). There is no Category 3 FN-DSA variant.

questions.question: Why is FN-DSA key generation significantly slower than ML-DSA?

options.text: FN-DSA must construct an NTRU trapdoor with specific properties
FN-DSA uses more hash function calls

questions.explanation: FN-DSA key generation must construct an NTRU trapdoor - finding short polynomials (f, g, F, G) satisfying fG - gF = q with good Gram-Schmidt properties. This is computationally intensive compared to ML-DSA's simple matrix sampling.

questions.question: Which operation is fastest in FN-DSA compared to other PQC signature schemes?
questions.explanation: FN-DSA has the fastest verification among NIST PQC signatures (~50 μs for FN-DSA-512). Verification only requires polynomial multiplication and norm checking, making it ideal for verify-heavy workloads.

questions.question: Why does FN-DSA have variable signature sizes?

options.text: The message length affects signature size

questions.explanation: FN-DSA uses Golomb-Rice compression to encode signature coefficients. This variable-length encoding produces different output sizes depending on the actual coefficient values, leading to signatures between ~666-809 bytes for FN-DSA-512.

questions.question: FN-DSA offers a NIST Category 3 parameter set between 512 and 1024.
questions.explanation: FN-DSA only defines two parameter sets: FN-DSA-512 (Category 1) and FN-DSA-1024 (Category 5). There is no Category 3 option due to FFT constraints requiring power-of-2 dimensions. For Category 3, use ML-DSA-65 or overprovision with FN-DSA-1024.
